primary_objective: Reduce Day-1 through Day-7 abandonment from ~75% to below 40% by redesigning the onboarding and first-session experience with persistent AI memory, behavioral retention mechanics, and a personalization loop that delivers perceived value before the user has a reason to quit.
critical_constraints: Solo founder — zero parallel workstreams without automation or async tooling
AI infrastructure hard capped at $200/month — no brute-force LLM usage; every inference must earn its cost
No persistent agent memory today — longitudinal personalization is architecturally blocked until solved
No behavioral economics layer — retention mechanics must be designed from scratch during MVP redesign
Cold-start data gap — AI cannot personalize meaningfully until sufficient early signal is collected per user
No design or growth headcount — every UX and acquisition decision is founder-executed
success_definition: Day-7 retention rate improves from ~25% baseline to 60% or higher within two iteration cycles
First-session completion rate exceeds 75% — users finish what they start on Day 1
AI coaching context persists across sessions — zero context resets in production after memory layer ships
Behavioral trigger system is live — at minimum one commitment device and one variable reward mechanic are measurably influencing return visits
AI cost per active user remains under $0.50/month within the $200/month ceiling
Cold-start personalization delivers a differentiated workout recommendation within the first session using minimal input signals

value_pools.audience: Motivated but inconsistent fitness app users
value_pools.description: Adaptive plans that adjust to actual performance and schedule — eliminating the guilt and friction of falling behind a rigid program

value_pools.audience: Users who cannot afford a personal trainer
value_pools.description: Personal-trainer-quality responsiveness and progressive programming at a fraction of the cost via AI coaching
Longitudinal performance insights, recovery pattern recognition, and progressive overload visibility that generic apps do not surface
Behavioral accountability structures — commitment devices, streak mechanics, loss aversion nudges — that make quitting feel costlier than continuing
Automated feature prioritization and data-driven iteration signals that reduce the cognitive load of solo product decision-making

core_questions: What is the single highest-leverage intervention in the Day-1 experience that would prevent the majority of immediate abandonment — is it onboarding friction, perceived value delay, or motivational misalignment?
What is the minimum viable memory architecture that enables persistent AI coaching context within the $200/month AI budget constraint?
Which behavioral economics mechanisms — commitment devices, variable rewards, loss aversion — are most implementable solo and most validated for fitness retention specifically?
How do we solve the cold-start personalization problem to deliver a meaningfully individualized first workout before sufficient longitudinal data exists?
What is the fastest instrumentation approach to generate automated, data-driven feature prioritization signals without a dedicated analytics or growth function?
How does TransformFit articulate a defensible AI value proposition to users within the first session, given that competitors like Fitbod and Future have established AI-adjacent positioning?
